mappings.rationaleAI: Washington covers the Declaration of Independence, command of the Continental Army, Yorktown, the Constitutional Convention, and his precedent-setting presidency, directly matching A.1.a on the Revolutionary period and A.1.b on strengthening the national government.

The War of 1812 covers Madison's administration, impressment, Canadian expansion ambitions, Tecumseh, and the Treaty of Ghent, directly matching A.1.b.iii on the War of 1812 and C.2.a on Canada's participation.
The Mexican-American War (1846-48) covers annexation of Texas, Manifest Destiny, and acquisition of California and New Mexico, directly matching A.2.d on westward expansionism and the Mexican War.

The American Populist movement covers farmers' grievances, the People's Party, Bryan's 1896 campaign, and Populism's influence on Progressive reforms, directly matching B.2.f.v on the Farmers' Alliances and Populist Party and B.3.b on the Progressive Movement.

Frederick Douglass covers the abolitionist movement, his relationship with Lincoln, advocacy during the Civil War, and Reconstruction-era politics, directly matching A.3.a on the Abolitionist movement and A.2.c on the Age of Reform.
The Lancashire Cotton Famine covers the disruption of cotton supplies during the Civil War and its global economic impact, relating to A.3.c.iii on foreign affairs of the Union and Confederacy.

Grant's presidency covers Reconstruction, enforcement of the 14th and 15th Amendments, fighting the KKK, and the Panic of 1873, directly matching B.1.a-b on Radical Reconstruction and Grant's administrations.

Uncle Tom's Cabin intensified Northern opposition to slavery before the Civil War, directly matching A.3.a on literature of the period related to sectionalism and slavery and A.2.e on cultural development.
The suffragist movement covers women's voting rights from the Seneca Falls Convention (1848) through the achievement of suffrage, relating to the Progressive Movement under B.3.b.

The Franco-American Alliance of 1778 covers France's entry into the Revolutionary War and the decisive role at Yorktown, directly matching A.1.a.ii on French military support in the War of Independence.

The Haymarket Affair of 1886 covers the eight-hour day movement, labour radicalism, and the Haymarket Riot, directly matching B.2.e on emergence of national labour unions and the Haymarket Riot (1886).
